texto: When Manya read, there was no waking her from her absorption; she heard nothing. A whole household might plan to tease and make a noise like all the zoos let loose with tin cans to play with and yet Manya wouldn't hear till her book was done. That was concentration and it was a joyful gift to have seized from the lucky-bag of life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eleanor Doorly: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “When Manya read, there was no waking her from her absorption; she heard nothing. A whole househol…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, blessings, concentration pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de concentration e reading-books.
